meta.title: Crux Control - Vendor-neutral AI agent control plane
meta.description: Discover, govern, route, and observe managed CLI agents and custom-built agents from one local-first control plane.

hero.subtitle: Crux Control discovers the agents already installed on your machines, governs their MCP tool calls, routes work across vendors, and gives operators one traceable command surface.

stats.title: The first Crux release is built around local agent operations.

features.title: Operate agents above the framework layer.
features.subtitle: Crux is not another agent SDK. It is the operational layer that sits above every runtime: inventory, execution, policy, and trace.

items.name: Discover and dispatch
items.summary: Scan PATH and native MCP configs, register managed agents, then dispatch jobs with context packs through the `crux` CLI.

items.name: Trace every run
items.summary: Capture subprocess output, execution status, trace events, cost estimates, and handoff records in one local control plane.

items.name: Govern MCP tools
items.summary: Inject the Crux MCP gateway so managed agents route tool calls through allow, deny, approval, and audit policy.

items.name: Unify custom agents
items.summary: Bring SDK-built agents through Crux-owned adapters and model gateways for deeper model-call observability and routing.

console.title: One operator view for managed and custom agents.
console.subtitle: Inspect a run, see the agent that handled it, review MCP tool calls, approve risky actions, and hand work to a fallback agent with the full context pack intact.

problem.kicker: The questions vendor SDKs leave to operators
items: Which agents are installed across our machines, and who owns them?
Which MCP servers and tools can each agent reach?
Can we route a job from Claude Code to Codex when the first agent fails or hits a limit?
Can we preserve enough context to hand work between agents without starting over?
Can we block a dangerous tool call before a managed CLI agent executes it?
Can we estimate cost from captured executions even when the provider auth lives outside Crux?
Can custom SDK agents share the same inventory, policy, and trace model?
Can we sync a local fleet into an organization-wide cloud control plane later?
problem.footnote: If agents are doing real work, they need a control plane before they become an unmanaged workforce.

pillars.title: Run agents like accountable infrastructure.
pillars.subtitle: Crux organizes the operational layer: inventory, governance, portability, observability, and release discipline across vendors and frameworks.

items.name: Inventory
items.summary: A single registry for managed CLI agents, custom SDK agents, MCP servers, model routes, owners, and runtime status.

items.name: Governance
items.summary: Policy for tool calls, approvals, budget limits, provider access, and prompt-injection scanning at the gateway boundary.

items.name: Portability
items.summary: Context packs and state checkpoints let work move across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and custom agents.

items.name: Observability
items.summary: Execution traces, MCP decisions, stdout/stderr captures, cost estimates, and AgBOM records in one queryable store.

items.name: Release Management
items.summary: Version policies, adapters, MCP server definitions, and agent profiles before they are promoted across a fleet.

products.title: One control plane. Six operating surfaces.
products.subtitle: Start with the local `crux` CLI and grow into gateways, fleet dashboards, Kubernetes, and cloud synchronization without changing the operating model.

items.name: Crux Console
items.summary: Web console for fleet inventory, executions, traces, approvals, MCP policy, AgBOM, and cost views.

items.name: Crux MCP Registry
items.summary: Registry of discovered MCP servers, tool schemas, trust scores, version pins, and policy state.

items.name: Crux Operator
items.summary: Kubernetes operator for reconciling agents, gateways, policies, executions, and traces as resources.

items.name: Crux Cloud
items.summary: Hosted organization control plane for fleet enrollment, retention, RBAC, SSO, and approval routing.

items.name: Crux Enterprise
items.summary: Self-hosted hardened distribution for regulated, air-gapped, or dedicated-control-plane deployments.

items.name: Crux MCP Catalog
items.summary: Curated MCP servers and tool definitions with signing, review metadata, and policy templates.

integrations.kicker: Bring any agent. Keep its native auth.
integrations.title: Vendor-neutral by design.
integrations.subtitle: Crux sits above your installed CLIs, SDK-built agents, providers, and MCP servers. It governs the operational boundary without replacing the framework.

items.name: Agent Discovery
items.summary: Find installed CLI agents and custom agents, import their metadata, and track owners in one fleet.

items.name: MCP Governance
items.summary: Proxy MCP tool calls through allowlists, blocks, approvals, audit trails, and prompt-injection scans.

items.name: Fallback Routing
items.summary: Hand a job and its packed context from one agent to the next when a run fails, stalls, or reaches limits.

items.name: Cost Awareness
items.summary: Estimate and attribute execution cost from captured traces without taking over provider authentication.

items.name: Fleet Profiles
items.summary: Export, import, and promote known-good agent, policy, MCP, and model-route profiles across machines.

items.name: Human Approvals
items.summary: Queue risky tool calls for review and preserve the decision in the execution trace.

pricing.title: Open-core. Operations are the paid surface.
pricing.subtitle: The Apache-2.0 core is complete enough to self-host. Commercial tiers add hosted operations, retention, org controls, support, and fleet-scale compliance.

tiers.name: Open Source
tiers.summary: Local Crux CLI, server, worker, gateway, MCP governance, console, adapters, and exports.

tiers.name: Cloud Starter
tiers.summary: Hosted control plane for small fleets that need sync and short retention without running infrastructure.

tiers.name: Cloud Team
tiers.summary: The organization tier for shared fleets, policy rollout, approvals, SSO, and audit export.
features: Org-wide MCP allowlist and policy push

tiers.name: Cloud Enterprise
tiers.summary: Dedicated hosting, regulated retention, support, and hardened delivery for large or sensitive fleets.

pricing.footnoteBody: Crux Enterprise packages the cloud operations layer for air-gapped and dedicated environments with hardened images, policy packs, and compliance evidence.

cta.title: Bring your agent fleet under control.
cta.subtitle: Start with local discovery and execution. Add MCP governance, traces, approvals, and cloud sync when the fleet grows.

footer.tagline: Vendor-neutral control plane for AI agent fleets. Built by AgenticFleet. Open source under Apache-2.0.
footer.copyright: © {year} AgenticFleet. Crux Control™ is a trademark of AgenticFleet.

shared.finalCtaSubtitle: Install the open-source core locally or connect a fleet to Crux Cloud when operations need shared control.
shared.metaSuffix: Crux Control - AI agent control plane

console.name: Crux Console
console.tagline: See every agent. Approve every action.
console.summary: Crux Console is the operator UI for the Crux control plane: fleet inventory, executions, traces, approvals, MCP decisions, AgBOM records, and cost views.

features.name: Fleet inventory
features.summary: Search managed CLI agents and custom SDK agents by owner, kind, runtime, status, MCP server, or model route.

features.name: Trace explorer
features.summary: Replay executions with subprocess output, tool calls, approval decisions, handoff context, and cost estimates.

features.name: Approval queue
features.summary: Review blocked or high-risk MCP tool calls and preserve the reason with the execution record.

features.name: AgBOM viewer
features.summary: Inspect models, tools, MCP servers, policies, prompts, and knowledge sources attached to an agent version.

includes: Inventory, execution, trace, approval, AgBOM, and cost dashboards
Live updates from the control plane event stream
Export paths for OpenTelemetry, OCSF, CycloneDX, and SPDX
Dark and light themes with keyboard-friendly operations

registry.name: Crux MCP Registry
registry.tagline: Know every tool surface your agents can reach.
registry.summary: The Crux MCP Registry imports each managed agent's MCP servers, tracks tool schemas and versions, and gives policy a stable target for allowlists, blocks, and approvals.

features.name: Server discovery
features.summary: Read native agent MCP configuration and register server URLs, commands, schemas, and owners.

features.name: Schema diffing
features.summary: Detect when a tool signature changes before agents can use it silently.

features.name: Version pinning
features.summary: Lock high-risk tools to known-good versions or quarantine them across the fleet.

features.name: Policy targeting
features.summary: Apply allow, deny, approval, and sandbox rules by server, tool, agent, or team.

includes: Import from Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and custom configs

cli.out: blocked · approval required for existing runs

operator.name: Crux Operator
operator.summary: Crux Operator brings the control-plane resource model into Kubernetes for teams that want GitOps-managed agents, policies, gateways, and traces.

features.name: Control-plane CRDs
features.summary: Agent, MCPServer, Policy, Approval, Execution, Trace, AgBOM, ModelRoute, and Fleet resources.

features.name: Reconciliation loops
features.summary: Keep declared agent and policy state aligned with live runtime state.

features.name: Admission checks
features.summary: Reject unsafe policy, untrusted MCP servers, or drifted model routes before rollout.

features.name: GitOps-friendly
features.summary: Use kubectl, Helm, Kustomize, Argo CD, or Flux with the same Crux API model.

cloud.name: Crux Cloud
cloud.summary: Crux Cloud hosts the organization control plane while your agents continue to run locally, in containers, or in clusters. Fleet enrollment, policy rollout, retention, and approvals become shared operations.

features.name: Distributed fleet enrollment
features.summary: Connect local Crux servers and workers into an org-wide fleet without exposing inbound ports.

features.name: Org-wide policy push
features.summary: Roll out MCP allowlists, approval rules, budgets, and model policies across teams.

features.name: Managed retention
features.summary: Retain traces, AgBOMs, and audit records beyond the local SQLite store.

features.name: Approval routing
features.summary: Route risky tool calls through Slack, email, or console queues with full audit history.

includes: 90-day trace and AgBOM retention on Team

enterprise.name: Crux Enterprise
enterprise.summary: Crux Enterprise packages the commercial operations layer for dedicated, self-hosted, air-gapped, and compliance-sensitive environments.

features.name: Dedicated hosting
features.summary: Run the cloud operations layer in your cloud, on-premises, or air-gapped environment.

features.name: Hardened delivery
features.summary: Signed images, SBOMs, hardened defaults, vulnerability response, and controlled upgrade paths.

features.name: Compliance evidence
features.summary: Export audit, policy, trace, approval, and AgBOM evidence for regulated programs.

features.name: Advanced support
features.summary: Named technical account ownership, support SLAs, and custom integration planning.

includes: Airgap bundle and private registry support

cli.out: verified signature · images loaded · charts ready

marketplace.name: Crux MCP Catalog
marketplace.tagline: Trusted tool entries for governed fleets.
marketplace.summary: The Crux MCP Catalog gives teams reviewed MCP server definitions, policy templates, security metadata, and signed descriptors they can mirror into private registries.

features.summary: Server and tool metadata with capability declarations, security review status, and ownership signals.

features.name: Policy templates

features.name: Recall signals
features.summary: Notify operators when a tool should be upgraded, pinned, or quarantined.

items.name: No OSS paywalls

items.name: Cloud is operations at scale
items.summary: Commercial value is shared org operations: SSO, RBAC, audit export, retention, support, and fleet sync.

faq.q: Is the open-source tier enough for production?
faq.a: Yes. The Apache-2.0 distribution includes the local CLI, server, worker, MCP gateway, core policy, adapters, and self-hosted console.

faq.q: What counts as an 'agent' for billing?
faq.a: An agent is one registered fleet record: one managed CLI agent, one custom SDK agent, or one deployed agent profile.
Cloud Team includes execution and retention allowances, then meters overage by capacity tier. Self-hosted OSS does not meter executions.

faq.q: Can I move from Cloud to self-hosted?
faq.a: Yes. Fleet profiles, policies, traces, and AgBOM records export through the same Crux control-plane model.
Never. Provider authentication and token billing remain with the provider unless a custom agent explicitly routes through a Crux gateway.
